module.title: Drone Surveying — UAS for Land Surveying
module.description: A comprehensive introduction to unmanned aircraft systems (UAS) for surveying and mapping. Covers FAA Part 107 regulations, drone hardware and sensors, flight planning, ground control, photogrammetry, LiDAR, data processing, mapping products, volumetric analysis, construction applications, and accuracy assessment. No prerequisites — designed for surveyors looking to add drone capabilities to their toolkit.

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Introduction to Drone Surveying
Lesson 2: FAA Part 107 Regulations and Airspace
Lesson 3: Drone Hardware and Sensor Technology
Lesson 4: Flight Planning and Mission Design
Lesson 5: Ground Control Points and GNSS Integration
Lesson 6: Photogrammetry Fundamentals
Lesson 7: LiDAR Drone Surveying
Lesson 8: Data Processing Workflows
Lesson 9: Mapping Products — Orthomosaics, DSMs, and DTMs
Lesson 10: Topographic and Volumetric Surveys
Lesson 11: Construction and Infrastructure Applications
Lesson 12: Accuracy Assessment, Quality Control, and Deliverables
Final Exam: Drone Surveying Comprehensive Assessment

module.title: ACC SRVY 2339 — GPS/GIS Applications in Surveying
module.description: Austin Community College SRVY 2339 (3 SCH). An introduction to Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS) and Geographic Information Systems (GIS) as applied to land surveying. Topics include GPS/GNSS fundamentals and signal structure, satellite constellations (GPS, GLONASS, Galileo, BeiDou), satellite geometry and dilution of precision (DOP), error sources and mitigation, coordinate systems and datums (NAD83, WGS84, ITRF), ellipsoids, geoid models and orthometric heights, static and rapid-static GPS methods, Real-Time Kinematic (RTK) surveying, network RTK and Virtual Reference Stations (VRS), GPS survey planning with mission planning software, post-processing with baseline solutions, the National CORS network and OPUS, GIS fundamentals including spatial data models (vector and raster), coordinate transformations, and integration of GNSS data into CAD and GIS workflows. This course provides the technical foundation for modern positioning technology used in professional surveying practice. Prerequisite: SRVY 1341. Textbook: GPS for Land Surveyors by Jan Van Sickle.

lessons.title: Week 1: Introduction to Satellite Positioning and GNSS Overview
Week 2: GPS Signal Structure and Satellite Orbits
Week 3: GPS Error Sources and Mitigation
Week 4: Coordinate Systems, Datums, and Projections
Week 5: Geoid Models and Height Systems
Week 6: Static GPS Surveying
Week 7: Real-Time Kinematic (RTK) GPS Surveying
Week 8: Midterm Exam
Week 9: Network RTK, VRS, and CORS
Week 10: GPS Survey Planning and Mission Planning
Week 11: GPS Data Processing and Network Adjustment
Week 12: Introduction to Geographic Information Systems (GIS)
Week 13: GIS Data Collection, Quality, and Analysis
Week 14: Integrating GNSS Data with CAD and GIS Workflows
Week 15: GNSS Best Practices and Comprehensive Review
Week 16: Final Exam

module.title: ACC SRVY 2341 — Advanced Surveying
module.description: Austin Community College SRVY 2341 (3 SCH). Advanced topics in surveying building on the foundational skills from SRVY 1301, 1335, and 1341. Topics include horizontal curve design and computation (simple, compound, and reverse curves), vertical curve design and computation (crest and sag curves), route surveying and alignment design, earthwork volume computation (average end area, prismoidal formula), construction staking procedures (slope staking, blue tops, offset stakes), topographic surveying methods and contour mapping, state plane coordinate systems and ground-to-grid conversions, introduction to photogrammetry and remote sensing, and survey project management. This course prepares students for professional field practice in construction and route surveying. Prerequisites: SRVY 1341 and SRVY 1335. Textbook: Elementary Surveying by Ghilani or Surveying with Construction Applications by Kavanagh.

lessons.title: Week 1: Course Overview and Review of Coordinate Geometry
Week 2: Simple Horizontal Curves — Terminology and Geometry
Week 3: Simple Horizontal Curves — Stakeout and Layout
Week 4: Compound and Reverse Curves
Week 5: Vertical Curves — Theory and Computation
Week 6: Vertical Curves — Design and Stakeout
Week 7: Route Surveying and Alignment Design
Week 8: Midterm Exam
Week 9: Earthwork Volumes — Cross Sections and Computation
Week 10: Construction Staking Procedures
Week 11: Topographic Surveying Methods
Week 12: State Plane Coordinate Systems and Map Projections
Week 13: Introduction to Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
Week 14: Survey Project Management and Professional Practice
Week 15: Comprehensive Review and SIT/RPLS Exam Preparation
Week 16: Final Exam

module.title: ACC SRVY 2344 — Surveying Legal Principles II
module.description: Austin Community College SRVY 2344 (3 SCH). Continuation of SRVY 2343 with advanced study of boundary law and the surveyor's role in complex boundary problems. Topics include advanced deed analysis with multiple conveyances, simultaneous and sequential conveyance conflicts, complex riparian and littoral boundary issues including the Red River boundary and tidal boundaries, mineral rights and the severed estate in Texas, eminent domain and condemnation survey procedures, subdivision platting law and municipal regulations, the ALTA/NSPS Land Title Survey in depth (Table A items, certification, exceptions), Texas Administrative Code Title 22 Part 29 (TBPELS rules for surveyors), advanced Texas case law analysis, writing professional boundary opinions and expert reports, and comprehensive SIT/RPLS exam preparation for the legal principles sections. Prerequisite: SRVY 2343. Textbooks: Evidence and Procedures for Boundary Location by Brown/Robillard/Wilson; Clark on Surveying and Boundaries (selected chapters).

lessons.title: Week 1: Review of Legal Principles I and Advanced Deed Analysis
Week 2: Simultaneous and Sequential Conveyances
Week 3: Advanced Boundary Conflict Resolution
Week 4: Complex Riparian and Littoral Boundaries
Week 5: Mineral Rights and the Severed Estate
Week 6: Eminent Domain and Condemnation Surveys
Week 7: Subdivision Platting Law and Municipal Regulations
Week 8: Midterm Exam
Week 9: ALTA/NSPS Land Title Surveys — Standards and Requirements
Week 10: ALTA/NSPS Table A Optional Items
Week 11: Texas Administrative Code — TBPELS Rules for Surveyors
Week 12: Advanced Texas Case Law Analysis
Week 13: Writing Boundary Opinions and Expert Reports
Week 14: SIT/RPLS Exam Preparation — Legal Principles
Week 15: Comprehensive Review and Integration
Week 16: Final Exam
